title: Project Metadata, Assets, PyInstaller 6.21, Platforms, and Clean Launch · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for project metadata, assets, pyinstaller 6.21, platforms, and clean launch and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, pygame-ce, SDL, display, input, audio, filesystem, packaging, GPU, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for portable inspected game release candidate. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Declare Python and dependency bounds, entry point, package data, license, authorship, version, and reproducible environment metadata.
Resolve packaged resources through supported APIs instead of current working directory or mutable source paths.
Build with PyInstaller on each target OS, inspect analysis and contents, handle data and native libraries, and test one-folder before one-file.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Project Metadata · changed case · Quiz Project Metadata
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 6672 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to package code and licensed assets from an identified revision and prove clean offline launch on supported platforms; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Declare Python and dependency bounds, entry point, package data, license, authorship, version, and reproducible environment metadata.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Asset Licenses
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Asset Licenses: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 4339 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to package code and licensed assets from an identified revision and prove clean offline launch on supported platforms; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Inventory origin, permission, transformation, attribution, package inclusion, fallback, and removal for every asset.

content.title: Evidence defense
